behaviors.description: Products marked as out-of-stock are excluded from the recommendation results regardless of their relevance score.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the handoff between inventory filtering and ranking: a high-score out-of-stock item can leak through only when modules are wired together. Rejected System because behavior does not require real OS/network/subprocess; in-process modules + local data reproduce it. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior is deterministic exclusion logic, not model-output quality or multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Wire recommender pipeline with catalog + stock state + ranker. Seed an out-of-stock product with highest relevance and verify final returned list excludes it after full pipeline execution.

behaviors.description: The user preference filter correctly excludes product categories the user has explicitly blocked in their settings.
behaviors.justification: Bug appears at module boundary between user-settings retrieval and category filter application. Rejected Unit because behavior is about explicit blocked settings being applied, not just standalone predicate logic. Rejected System: no real OS/network requirement to expose this failure. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI-output dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Run recommendation request with persisted blocked categories and mixed-category candidates; assert blocked categories are absent in final recommendations.

behaviors.description: The recommendation algorithm ranks products that match the user's recent browsing patterns higher than products with no browsing signal.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is core scoring/ranking logic for one algorithmic boundary and can fail without module interaction. Rejected Integration because no required cross-module handoff is implied by this behavior text. Rejected Agentic: text describes ranking rule, not real ML output quality requirement or non-deterministic model call.
behaviors.test_description: Call ranking/scoring function with controlled feature vectors where only browsing-signal differs; assert item with recent-browsing match gets higher score/rank than item with zero browsing signal.

behaviors.description: Pagination returns the correct page of results, with stable ordering — requesting page 2 twice returns identical results if the underlying data hasn't changed.
behaviors.justification: Stability depends on ordering + pagination modules composed together; isolated unit tests may miss unstable tie-ordering across boundaries. Rejected System because no real OS/network/subprocess is needed to reproduce page-stability failures. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because determinism here is standard software ordering determinism, not LLM non-determinism.
behaviors.test_description: Execute full query->sort->paginate flow on fixed dataset with ties; request page 2 twice without state changes and assert identical item IDs and order.

behaviors.description: The recommendation algorithm surfaces at least one product from a category the user hasn't browsed before (serendipity requirement), avoiding a filter bubble.
behaviors.justification: Serendipity is enforced by interaction between user-history features and diversification/reranking logic; failure often appears only in composed ranking pipeline. Rejected System because no real infrastructure is required. Rejected Agentic: requirement is a deterministic business constraint (at least one unseen category), not model-judgment quality from a real AI call.
behaviors.test_description: Run recommendation pipeline with history concentrated in one category and catalog spanning multiple categories; assert final top-N includes at least one item from an unseen category.

behaviors.description: A new user with no purchase or browsing history receives recommendations based on overall popularity rather than an empty result set.
behaviors.justification: Cold-start fallback requires orchestration between history detection and popularity-retrieval modules. Rejected Unit because empty-history check alone does not verify fallback data path. Rejected System because local in-process wiring is sufficient. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: fallback criterion is deterministic.
behaviors.test_description: Create user with empty history, run recommender end-to-end with popularity source enabled, assert non-empty results and that returned IDs correspond to popularity-ranked items.

behaviors.description: Click tracking records the correct product ID, position in the list, and timestamp for each recommendation the user interacts with.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is event capture + recommendation context + persistence payload mapping. Rejected Unit because correctness of position/timestamp often depends on request context handoff. Rejected System: no need for real external infra; local DB/in-memory sink can expose mapping bugs. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model decision dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Simulate recommendation impression and click in application flow; verify stored tracking record includes exact product_id, rank position, and request-time timestamp.

behaviors.description: The response time for generating recommendations stays under 200ms for a catalog of 100,000 products.
behaviors.justification: Performance SLO depends on real runtime/process behavior (CPU, memory, execution engine effects) that mocks hide. Integration tests with fakes may pass while real process exceeds 200ms. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because latency target is system performance, not model-output quality or multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run recommendation service in real process with 100k-product dataset on representative environment; measure end-to-end request latency and assert p95 (or specified metric) is below 200ms.

behaviors.description: When a recommended product is purchased by the user, the recommendation algorithm deprioritizes it in subsequent requests.
behaviors.justification: Requires state handoff across modules: purchase event ingestion, user profile/state update, then ranking application. Rejected Unit because single-function tests miss cross-module state propagation. Rejected System since local DB/event bus test wiring can reproduce this without real external infra. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: deterministic rule-based deprioritization behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Generate recommendations, emit purchase for one recommended product, then request recommendations again; assert purchased product rank drops below prior position (or below configured threshold).

behaviors.description: The recommendation results are deterministic for the same user state — requesting recommendations twice with no intervening user activity returns the same ranked list.
behaviors.justification: Determinism must be validated across full pipeline state and ordering, not isolated helper logic. Rejected Unit because equal-ranked tie-breaking and state reads can introduce nondeterminism only when components are composed. Rejected System because real OS/network are not required to trigger this class of bug. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior specifies deterministic software output, not real model non-determinism.
behaviors.test_description: With fixed user/profile/catalog snapshot and seeded randomness disabled/fixed, call recommendation endpoint twice and assert identical ordered product IDs.
